sutta: buddha monks there are these three forms of dominance what three dominance of self of the world and of dhamma and of what sort is dominance of self in this case a monk who has gone to the forest the root of a tree or a lonely spot thus reflects it was not for the sake of ropes that i went forth from the home to the homeless life not for arms or lodging not for the sake of becoming such and such no it was with this idea here am i fallen on birth old age and death on sorrows lamentation and wars on despair and tribulations i am fallen on ill dukkha for done with you perhaps so doing some ending of all this mass of ill may be revealed to me yet if i who have forsaken the passions by going forth from home to the homeless life should pursue passions still worse than before that would be unseemly in me then he does reflect energetic shall be my striving and my mindfulness shall be established unshaken calmed shall by shall my body be not turbulent my mind shall be controlled and one pointed thus making self predominant he abandons evil cultivates goodness abandons things blame worthy cultivates things blameless and keeps himself in perfect purity this monks is called dominance of self and of what sort mounts is dominance of the world in this case a man who has gone to the forest to the root of a tree a lonely spot thus reflects it was not for the sake of ropes that i went for from from home to the homely to the homeless life not for arms or lodging not for the sake of becoming such and such no it was with this idea he am i fallen on birth old age and death on sorrows lamentation and woes on despair and tribulations and fallen on ill or done with ill perhaps some ending of all this mass of you may be revealed to me yet if i who went forth from thus from home to the homeless life should indulge in sensual thoughts indulge in malicious thoughts in harmful thoughts great is this company of men in the world surely in this great company there are recluses and brahmins possessed of supernormal powers clairvoyant able to read the thoughts of others even from afar they can see me though close at hand they may be invisible and they can read my mind with theirs they would know me thus behold this glanceman here my friends who though in faith he went forth from the home to the homeless life it lives his life mixed up with things evil and unprofitable there must be devas too of supernormal powers clairvoyant able to read the thoughts of others even from afar they can see me they would know me thus behold this cleansement here my friends who though in faith he went forth from the home to the homeless life yet lives his life mixed up with things evil and unprofitable then he thus reflects energetic shall be my striving and my mindfulness shall be established unshaken calm shall my body be not turbulent my mind shall be controlled and one pointed thus making the world predominant he abandons evil cultivates goodness abandons things blame worthy cultivates things blameless and keeps himself in utter purity this month is called dominance of the world and of what sort is dominance of dhamma in this case among who has gone to the forest etc thus reflects well proclaimed by the exalted one is dharma seen in this very life a thing not involving time inviting one to come and see eating onward to be known for themselves by the wise now i have fellows in the righteous life who abide in knowledge and insight if i who am one that went forth under this well-proclaimed dhamma vinaya should dwell in sloth and negligence it would be unseemly for me then he thus reflects energetic shall be my striving etc thus likewise making dhamma predominant he abandons evil cultivates goodness abandons things blame worthy cultivates things blameless and keeps himself in utter purity this month is called dominance of dhamma these then are the three forms of dominance .
commentary: This suta is more for manga and these are the three ways and the buddha says that a monk can remind himself to strive one is based on the cell the other one is based on the world that somebody might know through psychic power that is lazy etc and the third one is using dhamma to remind him to strike the other thing i like to explain here is now and then we come across the word recluses and brahmins recluses and brahmins this is a translation for the pali word samana brahmana this brahmana is a cast in india in the days of the buddha there were four castles in india the priest cast was called brahmana and nowadays they are called brahmin islam and so during the buddha's time [Music] the brahmins were the priests castle then you have the warrior class the katya then you have the merchant class and you have the workers class the lower class these are the four classes so the the brahmin class they are a caste so if somebody from another cast renounces and becomes a manga he cannot call himself brahmana because that is only reserved for the brahmin caste and so any other manga who renounces uh is called a samana that is why our buddha is very often in the suta is called gotama the recluse that's a translation for samana gotama so that's what i have to say about recluses and brahmins now
